profile.name: Geospatial Data Retrieval Agent
profile.description: This agent interprets a user data request, matches it to the most suitable supported source or sources, and uses the handbook guidance for each source. It generates and executes download logic, then normalizes the output into usable geospatial dataset artifacts. When a request asks for multiple independent datasets, the agent decomposes it into dataset-specific sub-requests and returns all generated artifacts together. It is best suited for general-purpose data acquisition from supported external sources including CDC PLACES, NYT COVID-19 data, OpenStreetMap, OpenTopography, OpenWeather, US Census Bureau, USGS Earthquakes, Natural Earth, USDA ERS, and EPA Air Quality System.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Geospatial Data Retrieval
skills.description: Identifies the optimal data source or sources, decomposes multi-dataset requests into independent sub-requests when needed, generates specific Python downloading scripts based on technical handbooks, executes each script with up to 10 retry attempts, and packages the results into standardized formats such as GeoPackage, GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, Shapefile, or CSV.

constraints.spatial: Dependent on the data source selected (e.g., Census Bureau and USDA are US-only; OpenStreetMap and Natural Earth are global).
constraints.temporal: Dependent on the data source (e.g., NYT COVID-19 data is restricted to 2020-01-21 to 2023-03-23).
other: Code execution will retry debugging up to 10 times upon failure.
Requests for multiple independent datasets are decomposed into separate sub-tasks; each successful sub-task contributes one or more artifacts to outputs.dataset_paths.
Vector output is consolidated into a single GeoPackage by default when possible; explicit user requests for GeoJSON or other supported formats are honored when practical.
Must map to one of the explicitly supported data source handbooks.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language geospatial task to the agent. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution. A single task may request one dataset or multiple independent datasets.

input_datasets.description: Optional array of input datasets. Each item may be an HTTP/HTTPS URL, a server-accessible path, or a base64-encoded file object with filename, data, and optional mime_type.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response: 'URL' (default) or 'Encoded'.

primary_artifacts.description: Downloaded or generated geospatial dataset artifact. Multi-dataset requests may return multiple primary artifacts in outputs.dataset_paths.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model.

requested_skill.description: Advanced optional skill-selection hint. Most clients should omit this.

credentials.description: Provide one model execution credential in the request credentials object.

provenance.description: Includes llm_calls, tool_calls, token usage (input_tokens, output_tokens), duration, number of artifacts, and sub-task counts for multi-dataset requests in the response provenance section.

reproducibility.description: Returns the exact Python script used for download execution in the execution.code.script field, along with environment details and selected data source. Multi-dataset requests concatenate the per-sub-request scripts with sub-request headings.

validation.description: Performs agent-level self-validation before returning results, including artifact existence, requested output format, readartifact is readable with the right library such as GeoPandas, pandas, or Rasterio, output has records, schema, or raster dimensions, non-empty records or dimensions, and simple request-aware checks such as year, requested US state, and contiguous-US filtering when those fields are available.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile. No role-based access control is enforced.

operation_policy.description: Python code execution with up to 10 retry attempts per sub-task. Vector output is normalized to GeoPackage by default unless another supported format is explicitly requested. Data sources are selected based on technical handbooks. Returned artifacts are self-validated and validation results are included in diagnostics.validation.

review_policy.description: output_review_required is set to false. No human-in-the-loop validation is required.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the relevant research on geospatial AI agents.

extensions.description: This agent supports data retrieval from CDC PLACES, NYT COVID-19, ESRI World Imagery, OpenStreetMap, OpenTopography, OpenWeather, US Census Bureau, USGS Earthquakes, Natural Earth, USDA ERS, and EPA AQS. Each source has a dedicated technical handbook that guides the code generation process. Multi-dataset requests are decomposed into source-specific sub-tasks and returned as a combined artifact set with sub_tasks diagnostics.

data_sources.name: CDC PLACES
data_sources.description: CDC PLACES provides county-level, census tract-level, city/place-level, and ZCTA-level chronic disease, health outcomes, risk behaviors, disability, and health status estimates for the entire United States. No API key is required. Data portal: https://www.cdc.gov/places/tools/data-portal.html

data_sources.name: New York Times COVID-19 Data
data_sources.description: Cumulative counts of COVID-19 cases and deaths in the United States at the state and county level over time from 2020-01-21 to 2023-03-23. No API key is required. Repository: https://github.com/nytimes/covid-19-data

data_sources.name: EPA Air Quality System (AQS)
data_sources.description: EPA AQS provides daily and annual air quality monitoring data for PM2.5, PM10, ozone, NO2, SO2, CO, lead, and other pollutants in the United States. Data is available from the 1980s onward at monitor, county, CBSA, and state levels. Requires a registered email and API key. API documentation: https://aqs.epa.gov/aqsweb/documents/data_api.html

data_sources.name: ESRI World Imagery
data_sources.description: ESRI World Imagery is a web map service that provides satellite image tiles. It can be used to download tiles and mosaic them into a larger image. No API key is required. REST service: https://tiledbasemaps.arcgis.com/arcgis/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer

data_sources.name: OpenStreetMap
data_sources.description: OpenStreetMap provides administrative boundaries, street networks, and points of interest from community-contributed map data. No API key is required. Data is typically queried through the Overpass API: https://overpass-api.de/

data_sources.name: OpenTopography
data_sources.description: OpenTopography provides global digital elevation model data through an API, with resolutions ranging from about 15m to 1000m. Supported DEM sources include SRTMGL3, SRTMGL1, SRTMGL1_E, AW3D30, SRTM15Plus, NASADEM, COP30, EU_DTM, GEDI_L3, GEBCOIceTopo, and GEBCOSubIceTopo. Requires a free API key. API endpoint: https://portal.opentopography.org/API/globaldem

data_sources.name: OpenWeather
data_sources.description: OpenWeather provides historical, current, and forecast weather data. Historical data can go back about one month. API limits include hourly forecast up to 4 days, daily forecast up to 16 days, and 3-hour forecast up to 5 days. Requires an API key. API documentation: https://openweathermap.org/api

data_sources.name: USDA ERS Geospatial
data_sources.description: USDA ERS provides county-level and Census-tract-level geospatial data for the United States through ArcGIS REST services, covering food access, food environment, rural classifications, income, jobs, people, veterans, and SNAP participation. Data spans multiple releases including 2010, 2015, and 2019 food access data and the 2023 rural atlas release. No API key is required. REST services root: https://gisportal.ers.usda.gov/server/rest/services/

data_sources.name: USGS Earthquake
data_sources.description: USGS Earthquake is an implementation of the FDSN Event Web Service Specification and supports custom earthquake searches using a variety of parameters. No API key is required. API endpoint: https://earthquake.usgs.gov/fdsnws/event/1/

data_sources.name: US Census Bureau Boundary
data_sources.description: US Census Bureau boundary services provide United States administrative boundaries including nation, state, county, tract, block group, and metropolitan statistical area boundaries. No API key is required. TIGERweb REST services: https://tigerweb.geo.census.gov/arcgis/rest/services

data_sources.name: US Census Bureau Demography
data_sources.description: US Census Bureau demography APIs provide demographic and socioeconomic data such as population, gender, income, education, and race. An API key is required for more than 500 queries per IP address per day. Available APIs: https://www.census.gov/data/developers/data-sets.html

data_source_credentials.description: Some supported data sources require runtime credentials. Provide source-specific credentials in credentials.source_credentials. Do not put credentials in capability documents, URLs, handbooks, or committed files.

format.description: Object keyed by data source ID. Field names are expanded into handbook placeholders using the pattern {source_id}_{field}.
